expectations.text: A single TDD-*.md file exists in the outputs/ directory
expectations.evidence: TDD-vendor-inventory-sync.md exists at outputs/ path

expectations.text: Document is at least 1500 words
expectations.evidence: Document is extensive, covering 10 sections with detailed subsections, easily exceeding 1500 words

expectations.text: Contains a 'Problem' / 'Context' / 'Background' section
expectations.evidence: Section '1. Problem & context' exists describing $80K/month CX incidents and stale Google Sheet

expectations.text: Contains a 'Scope' section with both in-scope and out-of-scope items
expectations.evidence: Section 2 has explicit 'In scope' and 'Out of scope' bullet lists

expectations.text: Contains a 'High-level approach' / 'Overview' section
expectations.evidence: Section '3. High-level approach' with pipeline diagram and three adapter pattern overview

expectations.text: Contains a 'Detailed design' section with substantive content
expectations.evidence: Section 4 has subsections 4a-4h covering schedule, cursors, adapters, concurrency, processing, backpressure, data model, capacity

expectations.text: Contains 'Alternatives considered' with at least 2 alternatives and rejection rationale for each
expectations.evidence: Section 5 lists 7 alternatives (A-G) including Airflow, webhooks, IMAP, Kafka, bypass catalog DB, each with rejection rationale

expectations.text: Contains an NFRs section with concrete targets (numbers/SLOs)
expectations.evidence: Section 6 includes concrete numbers: p95 run < 5 min, 2x poll_cadence freshness, $500/month budget alert, 26h Vendor K staleness

expectations.text: Contains a 'Risks' / 'Failure modes' section
expectations.evidence: Section 7 'Risks & failure modes' has a table with 12 risks (R1-R12) with likelihood, blast radius, mitigation, recovery

expectations.text: Contains a 'Rollout' / 'Migration' section, addressing the shadow-mode period from the PRD
expectations.evidence: Section 8 'Rollout & migration' includes 6-week phasing table and 'Shadow mode mechanics' with shadow_mode flag per vendor

expectations.text: Contains an 'Observability' / 'Operations' section
expectations.evidence: Section 9 'Observability & operations' has dashboards, alerts table (PagerDuty), and runbook items

expectations.text: Contains 'Open questions' with at least one entry
expectations.evidence: Section 10 'Open questions' has 10 entries including catalog API idempotency, shadow-mode arbitration, discontinued SKU surface

expectations.text: No fabricated/invented URLs to internal systems
expectations.evidence: Only placeholder '<link to confluence runbook — to be added>' and example domain inventory-k@vendors.<our-domain> with explicit placeholder

expectations.text: TDD is structured as a scheduled pull-based processing design
expectations.evidence: Section 0 explicitly states 'Dominant pattern: scheduled pull-based processing' referring to references/tdd-scheduled-pull.md

expectations.text: Explicitly acknowledges and handles all 3 ingestion mechanisms (REST, SFTP, email) — does not collapse them into 'all REST'
expectations.evidence: Sections 4c.1 (REST adapter, 8 vendors), 4c.2 (SFTP adapter, 3 vendors), 4c.3 (Email adapter, Vendor K) each have distinct treatments

expectations.text: Proposes a per-vendor connector / adapter pattern (or equivalent abstraction) with shared core
expectations.evidence: Common 'InventorySource' interface with three adapter implementations; pull stages (fetch, normalise, diff, apply, audit) shared across all 12 vendors

expectations.text: Calls out the Vendor F 60 req/hour rate limit and how the design respects it
expectations.evidence: Section 4f dedicated to Vendor F: hourly cadence not 30-min, ≤55 calls per run with 5 headroom, chunked full-refresh across multiple hours

expectations.text: Discusses per-vendor cursor / watermark / 'since' state, and how it's stored and recovered
expectations.evidence: Section 4b 'Cursor / watermark' defines vendor_sync_state table with last_cursor jsonb, adapter-specific cursor semantics, and recovery via S3 raw payloads

expectations.text: Addresses no-overlap / locking between scheduled runs
expectations.evidence: Section 4d: 'Per-vendor lock: Redis SET NX PX', UUID-fenced; second Beat fire becomes no-op with skipped_due_to_lock_total metric

expectations.text: Defines a freshness SLO and per-vendor stale-data alerting (24h+ stale = alarm)
expectations.evidence: NFR target: 'last_success_at no older than 2 × poll_cadence (e.g. 60 min for a 30-min vendor; 26h for Vendor K). Breach = page on-call' via vendor_staleness_minutes metric

expectations.text: Addresses storage and rotation of per-vendor credentials
expectations.evidence: Section 4c.1 and 6/Security: 'stored in AWS Secrets Manager, one secret per vendor. Rotated per vendor's own policy; rotation is operator-driven, no automatic rotation in v1'

expectations.text: Has a concrete plan for the emailed-CSV vendor (e.g., dedicated mailbox, automated parser, quarantine for malformed)
expectations.evidence: Section 4c.3: dedicated SES inbound address, DMARC/SPF/DKIM enforcement, S3 archival, SES messageId dedup, quarantine to vendor-k-needs-triage/ S3 prefix, 26h staleness alarm

expectations.text: Designs the shadow-mode rollout phase (compare to current source of truth, dispute resolution)
expectations.evidence: Section 8 Shadow mode mechanics: shadow_mode=true per vendor, applied=false audit rows, daily disagreement report compared with Google Sheet, ops arbitrates; Open Q2 names the arbiter

expectations.text: Designs the audit log of inventory changes with source and timestamp
expectations.evidence: Section 4g defines InventoryAudit table with run_id, vendor_id, internal_sku, old_qty, new_qty, source_observed_at, applied_at, monthly partitions
